title: Exploring Vinho Verde: the articulate low intervention wines of Márcio Lopes
summary: Website: https://mlw.pt/en/ Márcio Lopes makes some brilliant naturally inclined wines from Vinho Verde and the Douro. He’s doing it in
content:
Website: https://mlw.pt/en/

Márcio Lopes makes some brilliant naturally inclined wines from Vinho Verde and the Douro. He’s doing it in quite a parcellated way: each year there will be around 50 different vinifications, from 200 different plots. He’s been making his own wines since 2010.

One of Márcio’s emphases has been to focus on old varieties, some of which are in danger of being lost. ‘It’s very hard work,’ he says, ‘but I’ve already discovered 15 varieties here in Melgaço.’

We take a trip out to a beautiful old vineyard, Quinta do Paçô. He’s been recovering this vineyard for the last five years, and is now in the process of buying it. It was planted in 1932 with mixed varieties. It’s hard to source vineyards here: they are tiny, and people hold on to them. ‘To find 1 or 2 hectares together is very hard,’ he says.

Paçô is 0.5 hectares, and it’s terraced. It also has other crops growing in the middle of it – very old school. He gets around 1000 bottles from here. The bunches from these ramada vines tend to be big and closed, so they can explode with rot: they are thin skinned. Viticulture needs to be attentive.

In Melgaço there is more metamorphic granite, he says, giving more concentration. In Monção the soils are heavier and the wines are fruitier.

Pequentos Rabentos Lote 3 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
5.5% alcohol. Alvarinho, Loureiro, Trajadura. Reverse osmosis. Fresh, light, lemony and tart with some herby hints to the crisp fruit. Light and tart. It’s a bit more expensive than the normal wine because of the cost of the dealcohlization process. 86/100

Pequenos Rebentos Loureiro Cuvée 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
11.5% alcohol. Sourced from 5 hectares of vineyards around 20 years old, from Ponte de Lima, Barcelos and Braga. 6 months in stainless steel, no malolactic fermentation. Lovely fruit intensity here: bright with good acidity and vivid lemony fruit. Direct, linear and incisive with lovely acidity supporting the bright fruit. 92/100

Pequenos Rebentos Alvarinho 2024 Monção e Melgaço, Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. 70% from Melgaço. Metamorophic granitic soils with small berries and strong skins. Low juice yields of 50%. No malolactic, aged on fine lees in tank. Powerful and intense with citrus, apricot and cherry, with a nice mineral undertow. This has quite an impact. Very fruit forward and quite serious. 93/100

Pequenos Rebentos Atlântico Red 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12% alcohol. This is a blend of Docal, Cainho Tinto, Pedral, Alvarelhão and others. 80 year old vines. Comes in with 14 g/l at harvest with 12% potential alcohol, and there’s usually 4-5 g/l of malic acid. Wild ferment 45 days with 50% carbonic maceration in chestnut vats, and 50% foottrodden. Both are pressed and joined together in used French oak, and do malolactic here. This is light red in colour. It’s supple and juicy with fine redcurrant and red cherry fruit. So fine and supple with nice tartness, showing real elegance and precision. Has a nice sappy edge. 94/100

Pequenos Rebentos O Caminho Alvarinho 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. Grapes from Melgaço. A combination of skin fermented (7 d on skins) with stainless steel standard fermentation, followed by 10 months in used French oak. This is concentrated and powerful with some spicy detail and a bit of structure, as well as wet stone minerality. There’s citrus but also a touch of apricot. Lovely texture as well as keen acidity. Very pure and fine, with juicy acid on the finish. 93/100

Pequenos Rebentos Vinhas Velhas Loureiro 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. A vineyard planted in 1989 with a small bunch clone of Loureiro. The idea is to work on the lees and this is fermented in barrel (from Puligny-Montrachet). This is very taut and fine, with lean citrus fruit and a touch of pear and green apple. Juicy and shows high acidity with a stony finish. Pure, linear, taut and quite lovely. 94/100

Pequenos Rebentos Azal Selvagem 2021 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. In flowering they remove a couple of leaves in the region of the flowers so the fruit set isn’t 100% in the cluster. Enforcada planted in 1932. Destemmed grapes, foottrodden then fermented in amphora for 30 days, then 9 months in used barrels. This is powerful, stony and mineral with keen acidity. This has some structure but also great acidity, with lovely tension and focus to the fruit. Really fine and pure with a long finish. 94/100

Pequenos Rebentos Touché 2022 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12% alcohol. Bastardo, Alvarelhão, Cainho Tinto and others. Single plot (we visited it) planted in 1937 in Melgaço. 50% foot trodden, 50% carbonic. Pale in colour. So fine, pure and supple with red cherry and redcurrant, showing nice tension with good acidity and lovely purity. Supple with some stony, mineral undercurrents. Very pure and fine with a pure stone finish. Such finesse. 95/100

Pequenos Rebentos Viagem Ao Princípio do Mundo Alvarinho 2021 Monção e Melgaço Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. Single vineyard Alvarinho from Melgaço, skin fermented for a month and then pressed and aged 18 months in Sherry casks with a flor veil. Powerful, linear, firm, nicely structured with a salty tang to the vivid citrus fruit. Such intensity with great acidity. Has some waxy detail with nice crystalline citrus fruit. Such a finish with lovely acidity. 94/100

title: Luís Seabra Vinhos: impressive wines from Vinho Verde, Douro and Dão
summary: Website: https://luisseabravinhos.com/en Luis Seabra is one of Portugal’s leading winemakers. He made his name as winemaker for Niepoort, before leaving
content:
Website: https://luisseabravinhos.com/en

Luis Seabra is one of Portugal’s leading winemakers. He made his name as winemaker for Niepoort, before leaving to set up his own project in 2013. Seabra’s idea was to make uncompromising wines: the sort he wanted to make, without thinking about what the market wants. He wanted to create authentic expressions of these regions. He sources grapes from top vineyards in the Douro, Vinho Verde and Dão. Winemaking is low intervention, with natural yeast ferments. All his reds are now 100% whole bunch, and he aims for low extraction, though leaves the wine on the skins for as long as 30 days, extracting very gently. These are wines of balance and finesse, and are worth seeking out.

I tasted these wines with winemaker Frederico Ferreira at Simplesmente Vinhos in Porto, February 2026.

Luís Seabra Xisto Illimitado 2024 Douro, Portugal
Transition soils. Stony and bright but with nice fleshy pear and peach notes. Textured and delicious. 93/100

Luís Seabra Granito Cru Alvarinho 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Alvarinho from Melgaço in foudres. Nice reductive tension on the nose. Fresh but ripe crystalline citrus fruit with lovely depth and a salty twist to the finish. Beautiful. 95/100

Luís Seabra Granito Cru 2024 Dão, Portugal
Mainly Encruzado and some Bical and Cercial. Barrels. Bold and concentrated with beautifully integrated oak and nice texture. Such finesse here. 94/100

Luís Seabra Xisto Cru 2024 Douro, Portugal
Single vineyard, transition soils. 600m mainly Rabigato. Barrel. Tight with lovely tension but also some sweet pear and peach fruit. Lovely depth but also focus. 95/100

Luís Seabra Mono M 2024 Douro, Portugal
Mourisco. Big berried variety. Lighter red. Perfumed with glacé cherry and pear, as well as stewed strawberry. Fun. 92/100

Luís Seabra Xisto Ilimitado Tinto 2023 Douro, Portugal
Concrete steel and barrel. Bright and lively with lovely fruit. Juicy raspberry and strawberry fruit with nice focus. 92/100

Luís Seabra Mono A Alfrocheiro 2024 Dao, Portugal
Lagar then tonels. Juicy and fine really elegant but has grainy structure under the juicy cherry and raspberry fruit. Lovely focus. So stylish. 94/100

Luís Seabra Mono C 2023 Douro, Portugal
Castelão. Just in concrete then big barrels. juicy sappy and vivid with tart raspberry fruit. So fresh and expressive. 93/100

Luís Seabra Delito Mencia 2023 Ribera Sacra, Spain
Fresh and juicy with sour cherries and good acidity. Taut but fully ripe. 94/100

Luís Seabra Indie Xisto 2023 Douro, Portugal
Single vineyard in Alijó. Tinta Roriz. Powerful and vivid with tart berry fruits, good acidity and firm tannins. Finishes fresh. 93/100

Luís Seabra Xisto Cru 2023 Douro, Portugal
80 year old vineyard field blend. Low extraction, then barrel. Juicy and refined with nice vivid raspberry and cherry fruit finishing fresh. Beautiful 95/100

title: United States of Blaufränkisch (3) Austria
summary: Austria has more examples of Blaufränkisch in this selection than any other country, and this is fair, because it has
content:
Austria has more examples of Blaufränkisch in this selection than any other country, and this is fair, because it has a very strong offering. This selection of 40 wines is pretty strong, and also diverse in terms of regions and styles. Are these the best 40 Blaufränkisch wines in Austria? That’s debatable, and we’re missing at least four of the top producers, Moric, Markus Altenberger, Weininger and Rosi Schuster. But it’s a very good selection, and it was a pleasure to work through them.

  • Part 1, Hungary, is here
  • Part 2, Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Germany, Romania, is here
  • Part 3, Austria, is here

Weingut Bernhard Ott Moritz Ott 2023 Wagram, Austria
Such nice density. Focused and fresh with lots of body and texture to the black fruits. Nice freshness here, with a sour cherry finish. 94/100

Gerhard Markowitsch Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Bärnreiser 1 ÖTW 2023 Carnuntum, Austria
Lively, spicy nose with some slight wildness. Lovely palate is supple, ripe and shows appealing cherry and berry fruits with some spicy wildness. Grainy and dense. 91/100

Weingut Artner Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Kirchweingarten 1 ÖTW 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
Supple and fresh with nice grainy structure. There’s some nice grip here. Good acidity under the black fruits. 93/100

Michaela Riedmüller Weine Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Braunsberg 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
This is quite elegant with a nuanced body, some nice spiciness and supple fruit. Dried herbs on the finish. Stylish. 93/100

Weingut Dorli Muhr Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Spitzerberg-Obere Roterd 1ÖTW 2021 Carnuntum, Austria
Fresh, supple and aromatic with beautiful balance on the palate, and nice fine spicy detail. Such poise here with black and red chery fruit and good structure. 95/100

Weingut Kerschbaum, Blaufränkisch Ried Dürrau 2023 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Assertive spice-laden berry fruit nose. Lots of oak on the palate making it quite stern, but the fruit is good. 90/100

Weingut K+K Kirnbauer Blaufränkisch Mittelburgenland DAC Reserve Gold Reserve 2022 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Focused black cherry and blackberry fruit with some sour notes. Lively and intense with some lovely fruit, finishing sour. Lively and intense. 91/100

Weingut Silvia Heinrich Blaufränkisch Alte Reben 2019 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Juicy and vivid with some spicy oak. Firm and full with blackberry and black cherry fruit. Vivid and concentrated. 90/100

Weingut Kerschbaum Blaufränkisch Ried Dürrau 2019 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Supple and bright with nice, lively blackberry fruit. There’s some spicy character here, too. Nice weight. 91/100

Weingut Claus Preisinger Blaufränkisch ErDELuftGRAsundreBEN 2023 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Floral and inviting with elegant red cherry fruit on the nose. Super-fresh, pure palate is elegant, bright, expressive and red fruited. 95/100

Weingut Juris Tricata 2022 Neusiedlersee, Austria
A big, rich, ripe, oaky style with lots of slightly charred black cherry fruit. Lively. 90/100

Weingut Juris Blaufränkisch Ried Ungerberg 2021 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Fresh but still ripe, with red berries, spicy oak and nice intensity. It’s likeable in its style. 91/100

Weingut Paul Achs Blaufränkisch Ried Altenberg 2016 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Supple and ripe with nice detail and some structure under the fresh berry fruit. A little stern. 91/100

Weingut Umathum Blaufränkisch Ried Kirchberg 2015 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Supple and juicy with some spice and nice red fruits. Bright and pure with some nice structure. Finishes fresh with a sour cherry bite. 94/100

Weingut Esterházy Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Schildten 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Lovely floral nose with some brightness and purpose. Beautiful freshness and concentration here with red fruits to the fore. Nicely textured, and shows good acidity. 94/100

Weingut Nehrer Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Poschen 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Juicy and supple with nice acidity. Stylish red cherries and berries, finishing bright and fresh. Lovely stuff. 93/100

Weingut Remushof Jagschitz Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Ripe and rich, yet supple, with red berry and cherry fruit complemented by warm spiciness. Generous, ripe and sweetly fruited. 92/100

Weingut Ernst Triebaumer Blaufränkisch Ried Oberer Wald 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Floral aromatic nose with red cherries and berries. Nice weight. Juicy and grippy with lovely structure. 94/100

Weingut Ernst Triebaumer Blaufränkisch Ried Mariental 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Fresh and juicy, and quite vivid, with nice grip. Lovely balance and focus here with some crunchy bite and lovely purity. 94/100

Weingut Feiler-Artinger Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Ruster Oberer Wald 2022 Leithaberg, Austria
Linear and structured with freshness. Substantial, classic style with some oak, and vivid, intense fruit. 93/100

Weingut Feiler-Artinger Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Ruster Ludmaisch 2022 Leithaberg, Austria
Spicy nose with some woody hints. The palate is vivid with nice sweet black fruits, some stern structure and a juicy, drying finish. 90/100

Weingut Prieler Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Pratschweingarten 2022 Leithaberg, Austria
Supple and fresh. Lively with some grip. Grainy and full with good structure, showing real intensity. Firm finish. 92/100

Weingut Gober & Freinbichler Blaufränkisch Ried Kirchholz 2021 Leithaberg, Austria
Floral and juicy, with brightness and freshness, as well as some ripeness to the raspberry and cherry fruit. Lighter in style but still has structure. 92/100

Weingut Stefan Zehetbauer Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Reckenschink 2021 Leithaberg, Austria
Aromatic, spicy black fruits nose. Sweet with some oak present. The palate is supple and juicy with nice graininess and lively balance. 92/100

Weingut Hans & Anita Nittnaus Blaufränkisch Ried Jungenberg 2021 Leithaberg, Austria
Juicy and lively with nice weight. This has real balance with lovely ripe berry fruit and then a tapering finish. A fresh, inviting style. 93/100

Weingut Hans & Anita Nittnaus Blaufränkisch Ried Altenberg 2016 Leithaberg, Austria
Warm spice-laden red berry fruits on the nose. The palate is supple with some wood spice and pepper framing the focused red fruits. 94/100

Weingut Gernot & Heike Heinrich Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Alter Berg 2019 Leithaberg, Austria
Fresh, vital and intense with lovely black cherry and blackcurrant fruit backed up by substantial tannin. Taut, reductive and intense, this is precise and mineralic. 95/100

Weingut Kollwentz Blaufränkisch Ried Point 2019 Leithaberg, Austria
Some spicy oak in the mix. A rich, ripe style but not jammy. Maybe the oak is a bit high in the mix here. 90/100

Weingut Erwin Tinhof Blaufränkisch Ried Kirchberg Gloriette 2015 Leithaberg, Austria
Supple and berryish with some evolution. Notes of herbs and earth. Mature and quite grainy. 88/100

Weingut Groszer Wein Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Szapary 2023 Eisenberg, Austria
Spicy, woody nose with sweet berry fruits. Earth and herbs here with some leafy overtones and a bit of wildness. 89/100

Weingut Thom Wachter Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Königsberg 2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Fresh and supple with nice acidity under the sweet red fruits. Berryish with some grip. 90/100

Weingut Thom Wachter Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Saybritz 2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Lively and balanced with sweet, focused red berry and cherry fruits showing nice grip. Has lovely sweet fruit but finishes fresh, with a slight saltiness on the finish. Stylish and vivid with a nice acid line. 94/00

Weingut Stubits Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Csaterberg 2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Supple and fine with nice brightness to the cherry and berry fruits. Has some grip. Lively stuff. 93/100

Weingut Krutzler Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Eisenberg  2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Nice freshness and intensity with a stern, spicy structural component. Juicy and lively with nice balance to the red berry fruits. 93/100

StephanO Das-Wein-Gut Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Saybritz 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Elegant and refined, but still showing good concentration to the black cherry and blackberry fruit. Finishes fresh with good structure and purity. 94/100

StephanO Das-Wein-Gut Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Weinberg 2021 Eisenberg Austria
Aromatic nose with some woody hints to the fresh red cherry and black cherry fruit. The palate is lively and full, and fresh and intense, with good acidity. 92/100

Weingut Jalits Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Reihburg 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Lovely fresh red cherries and berries showing nice brightness, and lovely focused fruit, and good acidity. Concentrated and tannic, but refined, with a nice mineral twist. 94/100

Weingut Wachter-Wiesler Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Weinberg 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Lovely focus and freshness here with some reductive hints but also nice black cherry fruit with some sweetness and also good structural framing. 94/100

Weingut Uwe Schiefer Blaufränkisch Ried Szapary 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Fresh and supple with a spicy overlay to the black fruits, and a grainy, slightly tarry edge and good acidity. 93/100

Weingut Uwe Schiefer, Blaufränkisch Ried Szapary 2014 Eisenberg , Austria
Supple and juicy with nice structure, showing some development with notes of herbs, earth and nice grip. This is really delicious. 93/100

title: Champagne Michel Gonet: one to watch, if they aren’t already on your radar
summary: Website: https://en.gonet.fr/champagne Michel Gonet have properties in Bordeaux, as well as their grower Champagne operation. They have a sizeable 38
content:
Website: https://en.gonet.fr/champagne

Michel Gonet have properties in Bordeaux, as well as their grower Champagne operation. They have a sizeable 38 hectares, spread out quite widely through the region, with vineyards in Côte des Blancs (these are Grand Cru), the Coteaux Sézannais, Montgueux, and Bar-sur-Aubois down in the south. Their winery and cellars are based in Avize.

Winemaking is in the hands of mother-and-daughter team Diane and Sophie Gonet, Chef de Cave Antoine Gomérieux. Marco Pelletier – owner of Vantre restaurant in Paris, who also makes wines at Bordeaux’s Domaine de Galouchey – is now a consultant here.

UK agent is Keeling Andrew

Champagne Michel Gonet 3 Terroirs Blanc de Blancs Extra Brut 2020 France
This was disgorged in 2025 after five years on lees with a dosage of 4 g/l, and shows a wide dynamic range, perhaps because it is from three very different terroirs (Le Mesnil sur Oger, Vindey, Montgueux). Great acid line under fine pear, apple and lemon fruit with some subtle toastiness. Really lively with a salty finish. 93/100

Champagne Michel Gonet Blanc de Blancs Grand Cru 2015 France
This is from Le Mesnil-sur-Oger, and the lieu-dit les Hautes Mottes. It’s zero dosage or up to 2 g/l depending on the vintage. Lively, fresh and linear with precise citrus fruit and good acidity. Lovely focus and amazing precision: this is really good. 95/100

Champagne Michel Gonet Blanc de Blancs Grand Cru 2016 France
Nice depth here, showing precision but also some weight. Lemon and pear fruit with some mineral detail. Lovely intensity and tension, with a long finish. 95/100

Champagne Michel Gonet Les Hautes Mottes Le Mesnil Sur Oger Grand Cru Brut Nature 2018 France
From the best part of Le Mesnil, this wine was initiated by Marco Pelletier and Raj Parr has also been involved in its creation. This site, next to Les Chétillons, is at the extreme end of the Côte des Blancs and has 30 cm topsoil and then mother rock. Base wine is fermented in oak barrels and then it spends five years on lees. Amazing depth with notes of toast, honey and spice alongside crystalline citrus fruit, with a slight saltiness. Real depth of citrus here with some white peach notes. Vinous. 96/100

title: Highlights: Casa Valduga Arte Tradicional Brut 2024 Brazil
summary: Website: https://loja.famigliavalduga.com.br/casa-valduga I’m in the lounge at Sao Paolo airport in Brazil, after an amazing few days doing a deep
content:
Website: https://loja.famigliavalduga.com.br/casa-valduga

I’m in the lounge at Sao Paolo airport in Brazil, after an amazing few days doing a deep dive into coffee at Fazenda Mio, which I’ll write up in full soon. I thought I’d try the only Brazilian wine on offer, and it’s an appealing and affordable sparkler. I’ve tasted quite a few of the higher-end Valduga wines before, but this was my first time with this less expensive one.

Casa Valduga Arte Tradicional Brut 2024 Brazil
This is a traditional method sparkler, 60% Chardonnay and 40% Pinot Noir, and it has spent a year on its lees. It’s fruity and bright and nicely aromatic, with sweet pear and cherry fruit with a touch of sweetness rounding things out nicely, and lovely vivid citrus notes. Juicy and quite tasty, with the focus firmly on the fruit. It’s not aiming at profundity, but pleasure, and it finishes with an appealing bitter hint that adds interest. 88/100 (in Brazil this retails for just over £10 equivalent)

title: United States of Blaufränkisch (2) Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Romania, Germany
summary: The second part of this epic tasting of Blaufränkisch takes me to Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Romania, Germany. Some
content:
The second part of this epic tasting of Blaufränkisch takes me to Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Romania, Germany. Some really nice surprises here. This leaves Austria for part 3, the largest contributor to this tasting.

  • Part 1, Hungary, is here
  • Part 2, Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Germany, Romania, is here
  • Part 3, Austria, is here

SLOVAKIA

Slobodne Vinárstvo Alternativa 2017 Small Carpathians, Slovakia
Bright, open and expressive with lovely raspberry and cherry fruit, showing real elegance and drinkability. Distinctive and lovely. 93/100

Magula Rosenberg Frankovka 2021 Small Carpathians, Slovakia
Slightly wild spicy, herby nose with dusty notes. The palate is lively and juicy with a tart finish and spicy wildness again. 86/100

Sykora Fine Wines Frankovka Modrá black edition 2022 Small Carpathians, Slovakia
Warm and complex with textured strawberry and cherry fruit. Shows depth and some structure. Nicely expressive with lots of personality. 92/100

Bott Frigyes Frankovka Modrá 2023 Southern Slovakia, Slovakia
Classic in style with lovely vibrant cherry and berry fruits with nice acidity. Supple and lively with a good acid line. 93/100

Strekov 1075 Frankovka 2019 Southern Slovakia, Slovakia
Very spicy nose that’s quite vivid with some wildness. Tart raspberry and black cherry palate with some savoury phenolic notes. 87/100

Csernus Frankovka Modrá Selection 2016 Central Slovakia, Slovakia
Nicely aromatic with bright red fruits. Supple and nicely dense with fresh red cherry and berry fruits, finishing juicy. 92/100

SLOVENIA

Marof winery Modra Frankinja Mačkovci SVL Lega  2021 Podravje, Prekmurje, Slovenia
Wonderful aromas of sweet spice-laden black fruits. The palate has nice structure and weight with good balance. Real vitality and balance here. 94/100

Roka Winery Kog Blaufränkisch SVL Lega 2023 Podravje, Štajerska Slovenija, Slovenia
Good structure and weight with raspberry and cherry fruit. Juicy and grippy with a nice spicy overlay. Real substance. 94/100

Vino Greif Modra Frankinja Črešnjev Vrh 2023 Podravje, Štajerska Slovenija, Slovenia
Supple and elegant with nice vibrancy to the juicy raspberry and cherry fruit. Some real detail and elegance here. 94/100

Hiša vin Emino Modra Frankinja Eminent 2024 Podravje, Štajerska Slovenija, Slovenia
So attractive with sweet cherry and berry fruit, some ripe strawberry notes, and an openness. Quite lovely in a fruity style. 93/100

Vinska klet Šuklje Lodoma SVL Vas 2023 Posavje, Bela Krajina, Slovenia
Good acids and tannins under the sweet cherry and plum fruit. Pure and balanced. Nuanced with real quality. 94/100

Vinska klet Šuklje Vrbanjka SVL Lega 2021 Posavje, Bela Krajina, Slovenia
Supple cherries and raspberries with some grainy chalkiness, and slightly tarry tannins. Pure with real vitality. Elegant but substantial, ageing well. 95/100

Winery Klet Krško Modra Frankinja Turn Premium 2021Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
Good structure here. Vibrant cherry and raspberry fruit with good acidity. Fresh, spice-laden and tannic. Really vibrant. 93/100

Winery Klet Krško Modra Frankinja Turn Classic 2021 Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
Sweet floral aromatics on the nose. Juicy and bright and vivid on the palate with good acidity under the tart cherry and raspberry fruit, with lovely freshness. 93/100

Verstovšek Estate Blaufränkisch Classic 2021 Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
Some spicy oak on the nose blends into the berry fruits. The palate is vivid with attractive raspberry and cherry fruit, as well as more spicy oak. 91/100

Verstovšek Estate Cru Silnica 2022 Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
Vibrant nose with aromatic berry fruits and tarry, spicy oak. The palate is juicy and vivid with bright cherry and berry fruits and more of the spice. 91/100

Vinska Klet Colnar Modra Frankinja 2023 Posavje, Dolenjska, Slovenia
Bright, juicy and vivid with nice tart cherry and plum fruit, showing good intensity. Fresh, finishing grippy. 90/100

Albiana Wines Modra Frankinja Alto 2019 Posavje, Dolenjska, Slovenia
This has a savoury, spicy edge to the juicy fruit. Some grip here. Slightly stern on the finish. 88/100

CZECHIA

Dva Duby “Vox silentium” 2018 Znojemská, Czechia
Herbal, open aromatics with sweet red cherry notes. The palate is supple and elegant with nice brightness but as real texture, showing some nice evolution. 94/100

Dva Duby Vox in Excelso 2018 Znojemská, Czechia
Lovely depth and focus with nice taut berry fruits, as well as notes of dried herbs, and some plushness. There’s also an autumnal leaf-like undertow. Lovely. 94/100

Plenér Frankovka 2023 Mikulovsko, Czechia
Beautiful floral dried herb and undergrowth aromatics. This is really fine. Concentrated palate with textured, elegant strawberry and cherry fruit, showing real finesse. 94/100

J. Stávek Frankovka VOC Lizniperky 2023 Velkopavlovická, Czechia
Really intriguing. Elegant, complex and herbal. Slightly unusual, but really delicious, showing nice texture and complexity. 93/100

Bystřický winery Frankovka 2023 Velkopavlovická, Czechia
Sweet, warm aromatics of stewed strawberries and cherries. Slightly sour palate but nice complexity. Unusual. 88/100

POLAND

Winnica Kamil Barczentewicz Dobre Modre 2022 Małopolski Przełom Wisły, Poland
Juicy, bright, fresh and focused with lively raspberry and cherry fruit. At the lean end of the spectrum, but with elegance and focus. 94/100

ROMANIA

Weingut Edgar Brutler Codru 2024 Crisana, Romania
Sweetly fruited and bright with good acidity and nice freshness and focus. Some spicy, herbal detail. Grainy, tarry finish. 90/100

Crama Nachbil, Blaufränkisch Reserve 2021 Maramures  Romania
Supple and bright with nice structure and acidity under the juicy cherry fruit. Finishes grainy. 90/100

ITALY

La Magnolia  Franconia 2025 Friuli-Venezia-Giulia, Italy
Supple and vibrant with nice elegance to the open cherry and raspberry fruit. There’s nice fruit sweetness here: almost carbonic in style. Supple and expressive with nice weight. 92/100

GERMANY

Weingut Rainer Schnaitmann Konrad Schäbischer Landwein 2024 Württemberg, Germany
Juicy, bright and fresh with amazing energy, but it finishes mousy. Not rated.

Weingut Karl Haidle Lemberger Trocken “Kalk & Ton” VDP.Gutswein 2023 Württemberg, Germany
Floral, expressive red fruits nose. Juicy palate with nice freshness and lovely red fruit character. 91/100

Weingut Schweikart Lemberger 2023 Württemberg, Germany
Very appealing with bright, fresh red cherry and plum fruit. Nicely drinkable. 90/100

Weingut Schweikart Lemberger 2022 Württemberg, Germany
This is fresh, supple and bright with lovely precision  to the appealing red cherry fruit, with a touch of cranberry. Good purity. 93/100

Weingut Dautel, Lemberger Cleebronner Michaelsberg  VDP.Grosses Gewächs® 2022 Württemberg, Germany
Juicy and crunchy but with some substance to the vivid red cherries, with nice bite. There’s freshness and purity here. Fine and expressive with more red fruit than black. 94/100

Weingut Rainer Schnaitmann Lemberger Lämmler VDP.Grosses Gewächs 2022 Württemberg, Germany
Fresh and vivid with lovely brightness and energy. Nice tannic structyre. Pure, grainy and focused. 94/100

Weingut Roterfaden, Lemberger Endschleife 2022 Württemberg, Germany
Lifted aromatics, and sappy red fruits with some herbal character. The palate is bright and fresh and focused with nice spicy detail, as well as some sour cherry. Has a natural vibe. 93/100

Weingut Bernhard Ellwanger Lemberger “SL” Großheppacher Wanne 2022 Württemberg, Germany
Juicy but with some spicy, peppery detail, nice floral intensity and some grip. Very stylish. 92/100

Weingut Beurer Lemberger Stettener Mönchberg  Schalksberg VDP.Grosses Gewächs® 2022 Württemberg, Germany
Lovely purity amd concentration here. Fresh, vivid and expressive with some grainy substance, and good grip. 94/100

Weingut Kusterer Esslinger Blaufränkisch 2021 Württemberg, Germany
Juicy and grainy with nice purity to the vivid black cherry and plum fruit. Good weight, showing freshness and focus. 93/100

Weingut Graf Neipperg Lemberger Ruthe VDP.Grosses Gewächs® (aus dem Reifekeller) 2017 Württemberg, Germany
Sleek red cherry and plum fruit. Grainy structure. A bright style finishing with some sour cherry notes. 93/100

title: The Arundel wines from Joaquim Arnaud in Portugal’s Alentejo
summary: Joaquim Arnaud’s family has a had a farm near Evora for more than 450 years. The name Arundel has English
content:
Joaquim Arnaud’s family has a had a farm near Evora for more than 450 years.

The name Arundel has English roots. William Arnaud (a descendant of the counts of Arundel) came from England as an escort of Philippa of Lancaster, a princesswho come to marry King D. João I. He stayed in Portugal, and Joaquim and his family are descended from him.

A mixed farm, they planted vines in 2001 to complement some older vines that they already had, and since 2010 have been making wines under the name Arundel. I tasted these wines with him at Simplesmente Vinhos in Porto.

Arundel Flor Rose NV Alentejo, Portugal
Centenary vineyard, with the wine raised under flor for 8 years. Complex, intense aromatics with tangy flor characters and some orange peel. Complex palate with aniseed and a hint of raisin. Lovely. 93/100

Arundel Branco 2022 Alentejo, Portugal
6 months in barrel, then 2 years in talha. Full yellow colour. Lovely focus and structure here. Concentrated and linear with a stony edge to the fruit. 94/100

Arundel Tinto T17 2023 Alentejo, Portugal
Red and white varieties, spending 2 years in talha. Lighter coloured. Complex red cherry and strawberry fruit with some oxidative characteristics. Sour cherry finish. 90/100

Arundel Tinto T18 2023 Alentejo, Portugal
Just red varieties from the same vineyard. Centenarian. Oxidative nose. Cherries and strawberries, some clay. Fine texture finishing with herbs and spices. 90/100

Arundel Great Tinto 2024 Alentejo, Portugal
Trincadeira from old vineyard, raised in barrels. Concentrated and vivid with bold blackberry fruit and a warm spicy finish. 91/100

title: Exploring Vinho Verde: Covela, who helped lead the region’s charge to more serious wine styles
summary: Website: https://covela.pt/en/ I’m back at Covela, after a 10 year gap. And it’s nice to meet with Rui Cunha, who
content:
Website: https://covela.pt/en/

I’m back at Covela, after a 10 year gap. And it’s nice to meet with Rui Cunha, who may only be here in a consulting role, but he’s been involved since the beginning, in 1991.

Quinta de Covela is in Vinho Verde, but only just: it’s a few hundred metres from the Douro, and is located in Baião, on the right bank of the Douro river, at the southeastern extremity of the Vinho Verde region. Here you will find more Avesso growers, whereas on the left there are more Arinto growers, but you can find both grapes on both sides.

When Rui began here in 1991, Joao Nicolau d’Almeida was the first consultant. João had trained in France and he advised Covela to plant whites, including Chardonnay, Sauvignon and Gewurztraminer. Jose Rozas was also consulted, and he said it was ideal for reds, so they planted reds also. For a long time Covela made just blends: two reds, two whites and a palhete. These were a combination of Portuguese and international varieties.

Covela is a beautifully situated vineyard, in a natural amphitheatre with 16 hectares of vines on terraces. It was initially established by Manuel de Oliveira, a famous film director and screenwriter who was Portugal’s Fellini.

In 2011, Tony Smith and Marcelo Lima bought Covela, and they asked Rui if he wanted to change anything, and he said, yes, I want to make an Avesso.

There are 16 hectares of vineyards, with Avesso and Arinto the main varieties, followed by Touriga Nacional, Chardonnay, Viognier and a bit of Gewurztraminer. In 2027 they are going to plant some Alvarinho. They currently buy a bit of Alvarinho.

Brazil is an important market for these wines. They have a good importer there.

In 2013 they bought Tecedeiras and the Boavista, but Boavista was sold in 2019 because there was a big crash in Brazil and Marcelo needed money. Tecedeiras is still being made.

The vineyards are certified organic. They are also certified regenerative.

THE WINES

Covela Avesso Classic 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. Lovely texture and focus here with bright citrus fruit. Lovely lemony notes with good acidity and a stony, mineral twist. This has some floral detail but also brightness and focus. So expressive and delicious. Linear. 92/100

Covela Avesso Reserva 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. First made in 2017, fermented in older oak (used Austrian oak from Stockinger, 300 litres). Taut, linear, nicely concentrated with some texture. This has good acidity and a core of lemony fruit. Focused and linear with real refinement and precision. Long finish. 93/100

Covela Avesso Nature 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. No added sulfites, unfiltered, does malolactic fermentation. They use some chestnut flour, too, as a preservative. This is quite distinctive with concentrated lemons, apples and dried herbs, with a sour cherry edge and keen acidity. There’s a bit of lift and some spiciness to the acid line, finishing with some grapefruit. This is really expressive and quite delicious. 92/100

Covela Arinto 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. This is stony, bright and lemony with nice precision to the fruit. Stony and mineral with nice tension. Has a slight smokiness under the fruit. Quite delicious, finishing lemony. Beautifully proportioned. 93/100

Covela Alvarinho 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Bought-in grapes from near here, and not organic. This is bright, lively and fruit driven with mandarin and apricot. Juicy and expressive with lovely fruitiness. Bright style, showing nice depth of fruit. 92/100

Covela Touriga Nacional Rosé 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Juicy and fruity with some strawberry and cream character, as well as pear and citrus and a twist of cherry. Very textural and quite creamy in style. 89/100

Covela Branco 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
In the past this was called Covela Escolha, now it’s just called Branco, and it’s the wine that put Covela in the map. A blend of the five white grapes planted at Covela. Fresh, lively with nice concentration of citrus fruit. This has citrus, pear and nice acidity. Lovely tension here with good acidity. Bold, concentrated, with lots of flavour, but very fresh and linear. 94/100

Covela Chardonnay Reserva 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. This is fresh and lively with some pear and white peach, some mineral-laced lemon and then notes of herbs, nuts and a touch of dairy. It’s quite tightwound and finishes slightly bitter. Oak isn’t obvious. 91/100

Covela Viognier Reserva 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. This is the first vintage of this wine. Really nice peach, apricot and citrus fruit with nice acidity, and some crystalline citrus on the finish. Good concentration here with a tapering, spicy finish. Really impressive. 93/100

Covela Avesso Edição Nacional 2015 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Powerful and intense with marmalade, orange peel, lime and some fine spicy notes. There’s a bit of waxy savouriness here, and a lovely taut acid line. Really nice intensity here. This has developed really well. 94/100

Covela Avesso Reserva Edição Nacional 2017 Vinho Verde, Portugal
This is powerful and intense with lime and herbs, showing some pithy density, good acidity and bold, savoury-laced fruit. Lovely intensity and depth here, with some complexity. 94/100

title: A quick appraisal of the 2025 vintage in Bordeaux
summary: I didn’t get to do primeurs in Bordeaux this spring. In fact I never do primeurs in Bordeaux because it’s
content:
I didn’t get to do primeurs in Bordeaux this spring. In fact I never do primeurs in Bordeaux because it’s a huge investment in time, and I think my views on baby wines that are still in barrel (and are thus very hard to assess) don’t justify the amount of time and expense that primeurs involves. So I usually wait until the wines have been in bottle a while and taste a year later at the Union des Grands Crus tasting in London.

But this year Wine Lister put on a mini EP tasting in London where a small number of properties bought over their 2025 wines, and this seemed like a good chance to take a brief glance at the vintage. These are my notes, but bear in mind this is a tasting of very young barrel samples.

If I had cellar space, and didn’t mind the cost of carry, this is the sort of Bordeaux vintage where I’d buy a few six packs.

Château Pichon Baron Pauillac 2025
84% Cabernet Sauvignon, 16% Merlot, with quantity a little down (104 000 bottles versus the usual 150 000 bottles of the Grand Vin). Heady, floral and aromatic. Silky and spicy; fresh and vivid. Lovely sweet blackcurrant fruit but also some good structure to balance out the flesh. Nicely textured with an appropriately grippy finish. 96/100

Château Phélan Ségur Saint-Estéphe 2025
65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot, 2% Petit Verdot, 3% Cabernet Franc. Vivid and intense with freshness to the blackcurrant fruit. Very Cabernet like with freshness and precision, and plenty of tannin. Beautiful. 95/100

Château Duhart-Milon Pauillac 2025
73% Cabernet Sauvignon, 27% Merlot. Sophisticated spicy oak on the nose. Fresh with nice energy and has some tannic crunch. But it’s very stylish showing freshness and purpose to the sweet black fruits. 95/100

Château Grand-Puy Ducasse Prélude Pauillac 2025
65% Merlot, 32% Cabernet Sauvignon, 3% Petit Verdot. Vivid, crunchy and tannic with such pretty, aromatic, forward blackcurrant and cherry fruit. Really fresh and delicious. 93/100

Château Grand-Puy Ducasse Pauillac 2025
66% Cabernet Sauvignon, 31% Merlot, 3% Petit Verdot. Yields were quite low (one of the smallest vintages in recent years) especially in the blocks with less clay. Concentrated, rich and dense, with a distinct spiciness. Lots of oak here, but also some lovely blackcurrant fruit. Nicely spicy. Grippy on the finish. 95/100

Château Grand-Puy Ducasse h. Pauillac 2025
69% Cabernet Sauvignon, 31% Merlot. This wine has been made since 2024 and it’s made from five historical blocks in front of the Château. Named after the hirondelle (swallow). Aged just in amphora (Tava). Pure, concentrated and intense with amazing density of blackcurrant and cherry fruit. So fine and expressive with lovely structure. 96/100

Château Meyney Prieur de Meyney Saint-Estéphe 2024
62% Merlot, 34% Cabernet Sauvignon, 4% Petit Verdot. Vibrant, expressive and pure with lovely fruit: black cherries and blackberries and plums. Really impressive: bright and vivid. 93/100

Château Meyney Saint-Estéphe 2024
66% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot, 14% Petit Verdot. Fresh and aromatic with lovely concentration of blackcurrant fruit and nice grip. Fresh and vital. This is really good. 95/100

Château Siran Margaux 2025
50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 41% Merlot, 9% Petit Verdot. Immediate and vibrant. Floral and pretty, but with some tannic structure and spicy oak. Quite delicious with lovely fruit. 94/100

Château Lascombes Chevalier Margaux 2025
70% Merlot, 27% Cabernet Sauvignon, 3% Petit Verdot. Pure, vivid and bright with blackcurrant and black cherry fruit. Vivid and dense, this is really expressive with nice intensity and some spicy oak support. 94/100

Château Lascombes La Côte Margaux 2025
100% Merlot. Floral and spicy with some expressive oak. Vivid and concentrated but very pretty with nice intensity. 95/100

Château Lascombes Margaux 2025
60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot, 5% Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot. Concentrated and structured with nice density and some tannic blackcurrant fruit. Firm, but also with lovely flesh. Very bright and pretty with nice precision. Real potential here. 95/100

Château Larrivaux Haut-Médoc 2025
45% Merlot, 40% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Cabernet Franc. Lovely freshness and intensity with bright, vivid fruit and a nice acid line. Juicy and grippy with real definition. 94/100

Château L’Évangile Pomerol 2025
86% Merlot, 13% Cabernet Franc, 1% Cabernet Sauvignon. Very aromatic with lots of spicy oak on the nose, and bright and vivid aromatics. Fleshy but bright on the palate. Vivid and really incisiver. 95/100

Château de Ferraud Saint Émilion 2025
71% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Franc. Vibrant colour. Floral and primary with lovely aromatic berry fruits. Fresh, crunchy and light with good structure and balance. 94/100

Château Laroque Saint Émilion 2025
99% Merlot, 1% Cabernet Franc. Pure, fresh, juicy and vibrant with lovely purity and focus, finishing with grippy tannins. 93/100

title: Jean Baptiste Hardy, making beautiful wines in Muscadet
summary: As well as doing several vintages overseas, Jean Baptiste Hardy spent some time working with Jean-Marc Roulot in Meursault. In
content:
As well as doing several vintages overseas, Jean Baptiste Hardy spent some time working with Jean-Marc Roulot in Meursault. In 2018 he returned to his family estate in Muscadet, in Mouzillon, and began farming the old-vine Melon de Bourgogne vines organically.

Winemaking is best described as modern traditional, and low intervention, with most of the ferments in lined underground concrete tanks, and use of eggs, old barrels and amphorae for élevage for some cuvées.

These are brilliant wines. UK agent is Keeling Andrew.

Jean Baptiste Hardy Éclats 2023 Loire, France
This is from 3 plots of 30 year old Melon vines, fermented and aged in undergound concrete tanks for 18 months. Fine and appley with elegant pear and citrus fruit. Such balance with tapering acidity, and a sense of finesse. 94/100

Jean Baptiste Hardy Fief de Chaintre 2023 Loire, France
Single-vineyard, massale selection, aged in a 400 litre barrel, amphorae and concrete eggs, then after a year transferred back to concrete for 6 months. North-facing plot, gabbro soils. Concentrated, fine and linear with lovely precision and weight. This textured wine has brightness but also balance. 94/100

Jean Baptiste Hardy Clos de Lozangère 2023 Loire, France
1960 planted vineyard, sand and gabbro soils. Supple with nice concentration, yet still delicate. Notes of pear and green apple. Textural and mineral with lovely finesse, showing nice purity and delicacy. 94/100

Jean Baptiste Hardy L’Aubier 2023 Loire, France
This is Chardonnay, made in barrels and amphora, and then goes to concrete tanks with the lees to finish off. Mineral and fine with lovely tension. It has pear and peach richness but really nice delicacy and focus, with real precision and balance. Fine and long. 95/100

Jean Baptiste Hardy Le Petit Clos 2023 Loire, France
This is Gamay made with a millefeuille of whole cluster and destemmed fruit, layered, and then fermented with an infusion rather than an extraction approach. Very fine, pure and herby with pure red cherry and redcurrant fruit, and some nice grippy structure. Pure and floral with amazing precision, finishing bright with some nice green hints. 94/100

Jean Baptiste Hardy Ponceau 2022 Loire, France
Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon, destemmed. Vibrant cherries and raspberries with nice grip. Pure and energetic, ripe but fresh. Linear. 93/100

title: Canada’s Okanagan and Similkameen Valleys (5) Quails’ Gate
summary: Website: https://www.quailsgate.com/ Quails’ gate are one of the most important wineries in the Okanagan, making top quality wines at a
content:
Website: https://www.quailsgate.com/

Quails’ gate are one of the most important wineries in the Okanagan, making top quality wines at a reasonable scale. They are farming 435 acres (176 hectares) now.

The land has been in the hands of the Stewart family for a long time. They came to the Okanagan in 1908 from Ireland, and started Steward Brothers Nurseries. In 1950 they moved to South Kelowna, and established the farm in 1958, converting it to vineyards in the 1960s.

In 2015 they started planting on the other side of the Lake, looking to make traditional method sparkling. They got the first grapes from here in 2020.

In Spring 2025 they had to replant 84 acres because of the 2024 cold spell, and in Spring 2024 they also had to replant 48 acres because of the 2023 cold spell. They have lost some of the other varieties and doubled down on Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, the two grapes they specialise in.

As a climate mitigation experiment, they have planted a test block with alternative varieties and disease-resistant crosses. They have L’Acadie Blanc, Blaufränkisch, Grüner Veltliner, Frontenac Blanc, Petit Milo and Petit Perle, as well as two Blattner varieties.

They are also trialling geotextiles on one acre, and are trialling frolight (infrared cables).

Like many Okanagan wineries they had a land-based licence, which after the frost they switched to a commercial license in July 2024. This has allowed them to make wines from imported grapes. So they bought grapes from Oregon, Washington and California, and these form the label Field and Flight by Quails’ Gate. And they are also using a label called Plume for grapes from Sonoma, Alexander Valley and Napa. They had already used this brand for a Similkameen Cabernet Sauvignon in 2022.

They were down 20% in tonnage in 2023 after the December 2022 cold spell, and then in 2024 harvested just 60 tonnes of the usual 1200.

We had a look round the vineyard and tasted with Rowan Stewart, son of the owner Keith Stewart. Farming isn’t organic, but it is thoughtful. The standard approach is one glyphosate spray early in the season and then they move to mechanical weed control.

They don’t use the Okanagan sub-AVAs, so everything is labelled Okanagan Valley. Of recent vintages, 2022 was the best vintage in the last five, says Rowan. Then there was a  small bit of winter damage in winter 2022, and then there were bush fires in 2023. The whites were mostly unaffected, and the Pinots aren’t showing too much smoke yet.

Chardonnay is one of their specialities. ‘We love Chardonnay and I think we do this amazingly here,’ says Rowan. ‘Estate Chardonnay is being pushed on quality and quantity. We’d love to do 25% barrels for this, but we’ve been pushed. We are looking at battonage tanks. These have a giant blade in the bottom and when it spins, it vortexes up. You can lees stir without oxygen.’

UK agent is Berkmann

Qualis’ Gate Chenin Blanc Clone 220 2023 Okanagan Valley, Canada
This is raised in concrete egg and neutral oak. Supple and bright with nice citrus drive and good acidity. There are some really nice textural qualities here. Supple and fresh with a slight chalky texture on the mid-palate, and slight salinity on the finish. 93/100

Qualis’ Gate Estate Chardonnay 2023 Okanagan Valley, Canada
This is textural and fine with sweet pear and peach fruit, with a hint of honey. Nicely textured with good acidity but also some depth and richness of fruit. Stylish with a nice dynamic range and a sense of depth and richness. 92/100 ($28)

Qualis’ Gate Estate Chardonnay 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
This has brightness and depth, with some pear, a touch of citrus and some fine peachy notes. There’s some pastry, a touch of honey, and fine spicy detail, with good acidity. Nice breadth to this wine, but also some focus. Just a bit of oak here, with the emphasis on the ripe fruit. 93/100 ($27)

Qualis’ Gate Stewart Family Reserve Chardonnay 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
This wine has been made since 1994, and is the most iconic wine of the estate. 100 NTU target, inoculated, but wild malolactic fermentation. This is bold and focused with crystalline citrus fruit and some pear and white peach. Nice concentration here with some spicy wood notes (around 45% new oak) weaving in nicely with the concentrated fruit. Has some savoury notes here, a touch of butterscotch, as well as focused citrus fruit. Long spicy finish. 94/100 ($49)

Qualis’ Gate Dijon Clone Chardonnay 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
The vineyard that made this went down in the freeze. 96, 95, 78 and 548 clones. This used to be a nursery site, and the YANs are very low. It’s basically a sandpit. A touch of nice reduction on the nose. Taut, nutty, slightly spicy and very refined with subtle oaky hints and some spicy detail. Really expressive. 94/100

Qualis’ Gate Rosemary’s Block Chardonnay 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
Mostly 95 clone, with some 548 (Musque clone), plus some 76 and 96. All barrel fermented. This is fresh and linear, but has a bit of grapey depth, and lovely crystalline citrus with some fine spicy detail. This has richness as well as restraint, with some finesse. Nicely textured. There’s some oak influence, but it enhances rather than distracts. 95/100 ($70)

All the Pinot Noirs are destemmed, with the exception of the reserve wines where they use  5-10% whole cluster, after lots of trials. They are moving from punch downs to pump overs because the tannins can end up being a wall if you aren’t careful.

Qualis’ Gate Estate Pinot Noir 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
Around 13 clones in this (they now have 16). In the past they have balanced the over-ripe fruit with some grower fruit from lighter sites where they were cropping a bit heavy. Now they tend to lease rather than buy from growers, as it is hard to get good Pinot from growers. Sweet cherry and plum fruit with some nice acidity. This has a nice juiciness with ripe fruit, and a hint of sour cherry and damson, finishing fresh and expressive. Nice bite on the finish. Tannins are present, and firm, but integrate well with the sweet fruit. This is really expressive with some elegance. 93/100

Qualis’ Gate Stewart Family Reserve Pinot Noir 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
When this was first made in 1994 it was made in a rich style, almost heavy, because they wanted to prove you could make great red wine in the Okanagan, which was something of a debate at the time. This is dense with nice concentration of fruit, showing cherry and plum fruit with a touch of sour cherry. There’s some spicy structure here and well integrated oak, with a lovely density to the wine. Big, bold and beautiful, without being clunky. There’s a lot of wine here. 94/100

Quails’ Gate Dijon Clone Pinot Noir 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
Mostly 828, with some 777 and 115. Supple, juicy and quite fine with open cherry and plum fruit. Elegant and refined with good structure, but the structure isn’t intensive. Real finesse, with a lovely flow across the palate, showing purity. Good acidity and nice brightness, finishing with a bit of grip. Taut and compact. This is lovely, and has masses of potential. 95/100

Quails’ Gate Richard’s Block Pinot Noir 2022 Okanagan Valley, Canada
This is the top Pinot. 777 and 115 clones, from two parts of the same vineyard. Wild ferment, with very fine grain barrels. Textured and refined with lovely red cherry and plum fruit, showing real purity. There’s some depth, but also texture and elegance, with some silkiness to the red cherry and plum fruit. Grainy finish. This is lovely, and it’s approachable now. 95/100

Quails’ Gate Ailm Reserve Brut 2020 Okanagan Valley, Canada
82% Pinot Noir, 18% Chardonnay. 40 months on lees. Compact and focused on the nose with sweet citrus fruit and a touch of pear. There’s a hint of mandarin here. Nice focus and energy, with lovely fruit but also a bit of toasty complexity. There’s an ease to this, but also a touch of complexity. A really good starting point. 92/100 ($85)

Quails’ Gate Ailm Blanc de Blanc 2021 Okanagan Valley, Canada
24 months on lees. This is tight and focused with some lovely citrus fruit as well as a bit of white peach. This has lovely crystalline detail with nice concentration of fruit, and a tapering finish. Very stylish. 92/100 ($65)

title: Exploring Vinho Verde: the articulate low intervention wines of Márcio Lopes
summary: Website: https://mlw.pt/en/ Márcio Lopes makes some brilliant naturally inclined wines from Vinho Verde and the Douro. He’s doing it in
content:
Website: https://mlw.pt/en/

Márcio Lopes makes some brilliant naturally inclined wines from Vinho Verde and the Douro. He’s doing it in quite a parcellated way: each year there will be around 50 different vinifications, from 200 different plots. He’s been making his own wines since 2010.

One of Márcio’s emphases has been to focus on old varieties, some of which are in danger of being lost. ‘It’s very hard work,’ he says, ‘but I’ve already discovered 15 varieties here in Melgaço.’

We take a trip out to a beautiful old vineyard, Quinta do Paçô. He’s been recovering this vineyard for the last five years, and is now in the process of buying it. It was planted in 1932 with mixed varieties. It’s hard to source vineyards here: they are tiny, and people hold on to them. ‘To find 1 or 2 hectares together is very hard,’ he says.

Paçô is 0.5 hectares, and it’s terraced. It also has other crops growing in the middle of it – very old school. He gets around 1000 bottles from here. The bunches from these ramada vines tend to be big and closed, so they can explode with rot: they are thin skinned. Viticulture needs to be attentive.

In Melgaço there is more metamorphic granite, he says, giving more concentration. In Monção the soils are heavier and the wines are fruitier.

Pequentos Rabentos Lote 3 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
5.5% alcohol. Alvarinho, Loureiro, Trajadura. Reverse osmosis. Fresh, light, lemony and tart with some herby hints to the crisp fruit. Light and tart. It’s a bit more expensive than the normal wine because of the cost of the dealcohlization process. 86/100

Pequenos Rebentos Loureiro Cuvée 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
11.5% alcohol. Sourced from 5 hectares of vineyards around 20 years old, from Ponte de Lima, Barcelos and Braga. 6 months in stainless steel, no malolactic fermentation. Lovely fruit intensity here: bright with good acidity and vivid lemony fruit. Direct, linear and incisive with lovely acidity supporting the bright fruit. 92/100

Pequenos Rebentos Alvarinho 2024 Monção e Melgaço, Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. 70% from Melgaço. Metamorophic granitic soils with small berries and strong skins. Low juice yields of 50%. No malolactic, aged on fine lees in tank. Powerful and intense with citrus, apricot and cherry, with a nice mineral undertow. This has quite an impact. Very fruit forward and quite serious. 93/100

Pequenos Rebentos Atlântico Red 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12% alcohol. This is a blend of Docal, Cainho Tinto, Pedral, Alvarelhão and others. 80 year old vines. Comes in with 14 g/l at harvest with 12% potential alcohol, and there’s usually 4-5 g/l of malic acid. Wild ferment 45 days with 50% carbonic maceration in chestnut vats, and 50% foottrodden. Both are pressed and joined together in used French oak, and do malolactic here. This is light red in colour. It’s supple and juicy with fine redcurrant and red cherry fruit. So fine and supple with nice tartness, showing real elegance and precision. Has a nice sappy edge. 94/100

Pequenos Rebentos O Caminho Alvarinho 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. Grapes from Melgaço. A combination of skin fermented (7 d on skins) with stainless steel standard fermentation, followed by 10 months in used French oak. This is concentrated and powerful with some spicy detail and a bit of structure, as well as wet stone minerality. There’s citrus but also a touch of apricot. Lovely texture as well as keen acidity. Very pure and fine, with juicy acid on the finish. 93/100

Pequenos Rebentos Vinhas Velhas Loureiro 2023 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. A vineyard planted in 1989 with a small bunch clone of Loureiro. The idea is to work on the lees and this is fermented in barrel (from Puligny-Montrachet). This is very taut and fine, with lean citrus fruit and a touch of pear and green apple. Juicy and shows high acidity with a stony finish. Pure, linear, taut and quite lovely. 94/100

Pequenos Rebentos Azal Selvagem 2021 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12.5% alcohol. In flowering they remove a couple of leaves in the region of the flowers so the fruit set isn’t 100% in the cluster. Enforcada planted in 1932. Destemmed grapes, foottrodden then fermented in amphora for 30 days, then 9 months in used barrels. This is powerful, stony and mineral with keen acidity. This has some structure but also great acidity, with lovely tension and focus to the fruit. Really fine and pure with a long finish. 94/100

Pequenos Rebentos Touché 2022 Vinho Verde, Portugal
12% alcohol. Bastardo, Alvarelhão, Cainho Tinto and others. Single plot (we visited it) planted in 1937 in Melgaço. 50% foot trodden, 50% carbonic. Pale in colour. So fine, pure and supple with red cherry and redcurrant, showing nice tension with good acidity and lovely purity. Supple with some stony, mineral undercurrents. Very pure and fine with a pure stone finish. Such finesse. 95/100

Pequenos Rebentos Viagem Ao Princípio do Mundo Alvarinho 2021 Monção e Melgaço Vinho Verde, Portugal
13% alcohol. Single vineyard Alvarinho from Melgaço, skin fermented for a month and then pressed and aged 18 months in Sherry casks with a flor veil. Powerful, linear, firm, nicely structured with a salty tang to the vivid citrus fruit. Such intensity with great acidity. Has some waxy detail with nice crystalline citrus fruit. Such a finish with lovely acidity. 94/100

title: Luís Seabra Vinhos: impressive wines from Vinho Verde, Douro and Dão
summary: Website: https://luisseabravinhos.com/en Luis Seabra is one of Portugal’s leading winemakers. He made his name as winemaker for Niepoort, before leaving
content:
Website: https://luisseabravinhos.com/en

Luis Seabra is one of Portugal’s leading winemakers. He made his name as winemaker for Niepoort, before leaving to set up his own project in 2013. Seabra’s idea was to make uncompromising wines: the sort he wanted to make, without thinking about what the market wants. He wanted to create authentic expressions of these regions. He sources grapes from top vineyards in the Douro, Vinho Verde and Dão. Winemaking is low intervention, with natural yeast ferments. All his reds are now 100% whole bunch, and he aims for low extraction, though leaves the wine on the skins for as long as 30 days, extracting very gently. These are wines of balance and finesse, and are worth seeking out.

I tasted these wines with winemaker Frederico Ferreira at Simplesmente Vinhos in Porto, February 2026.

Luís Seabra Xisto Illimitado 2024 Douro, Portugal
Transition soils. Stony and bright but with nice fleshy pear and peach notes. Textured and delicious. 93/100

Luís Seabra Granito Cru Alvarinho 2024 Vinho Verde, Portugal
Alvarinho from Melgaço in foudres. Nice reductive tension on the nose. Fresh but ripe crystalline citrus fruit with lovely depth and a salty twist to the finish. Beautiful. 95/100

Luís Seabra Granito Cru 2024 Dão, Portugal
Mainly Encruzado and some Bical and Cercial. Barrels. Bold and concentrated with beautifully integrated oak and nice texture. Such finesse here. 94/100

Luís Seabra Xisto Cru 2024 Douro, Portugal
Single vineyard, transition soils. 600m mainly Rabigato. Barrel. Tight with lovely tension but also some sweet pear and peach fruit. Lovely depth but also focus. 95/100

Luís Seabra Mono M 2024 Douro, Portugal
Mourisco. Big berried variety. Lighter red. Perfumed with glacé cherry and pear, as well as stewed strawberry. Fun. 92/100

Luís Seabra Xisto Ilimitado Tinto 2023 Douro, Portugal
Concrete steel and barrel. Bright and lively with lovely fruit. Juicy raspberry and strawberry fruit with nice focus. 92/100

Luís Seabra Mono A Alfrocheiro 2024 Dao, Portugal
Lagar then tonels. Juicy and fine really elegant but has grainy structure under the juicy cherry and raspberry fruit. Lovely focus. So stylish. 94/100

Luís Seabra Mono C 2023 Douro, Portugal
Castelão. Just in concrete then big barrels. juicy sappy and vivid with tart raspberry fruit. So fresh and expressive. 93/100

Luís Seabra Delito Mencia 2023 Ribera Sacra, Spain
Fresh and juicy with sour cherries and good acidity. Taut but fully ripe. 94/100

Luís Seabra Indie Xisto 2023 Douro, Portugal
Single vineyard in Alijó. Tinta Roriz. Powerful and vivid with tart berry fruits, good acidity and firm tannins. Finishes fresh. 93/100

Luís Seabra Xisto Cru 2023 Douro, Portugal
80 year old vineyard field blend. Low extraction, then barrel. Juicy and refined with nice vivid raspberry and cherry fruit finishing fresh. Beautiful 95/100

title: United States of Blaufränkisch (3) Austria
summary: Austria has more examples of Blaufränkisch in this selection than any other country, and this is fair, because it has
content:
Austria has more examples of Blaufränkisch in this selection than any other country, and this is fair, because it has a very strong offering. This selection of 40 wines is pretty strong, and also diverse in terms of regions and styles. Are these the best 40 Blaufränkisch wines in Austria? That’s debatable, and we’re missing at least four of the top producers, Moric, Markus Altenberger, Weininger and Rosi Schuster. But it’s a very good selection, and it was a pleasure to work through them.

  • Part 1, Hungary, is here
  • Part 2, Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Germany, Romania, is here
  • Part 3, Austria, is here

Weingut Bernhard Ott Moritz Ott 2023 Wagram, Austria
Such nice density. Focused and fresh with lots of body and texture to the black fruits. Nice freshness here, with a sour cherry finish. 94/100

Gerhard Markowitsch Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Bärnreiser 1 ÖTW 2023 Carnuntum, Austria
Lively, spicy nose with some slight wildness. Lovely palate is supple, ripe and shows appealing cherry and berry fruits with some spicy wildness. Grainy and dense. 91/100

Weingut Artner Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Kirchweingarten 1 ÖTW 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
Supple and fresh with nice grainy structure. There’s some nice grip here. Good acidity under the black fruits. 93/100

Michaela Riedmüller Weine Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Braunsberg 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
This is quite elegant with a nuanced body, some nice spiciness and supple fruit. Dried herbs on the finish. Stylish. 93/100

Weingut Dorli Muhr Blaufränkisch Carnuntum DAC Ried Spitzerberg-Obere Roterd 1ÖTW 2021 Carnuntum, Austria
Fresh, supple and aromatic with beautiful balance on the palate, and nice fine spicy detail. Such poise here with black and red chery fruit and good structure. 95/100

Weingut Kerschbaum, Blaufränkisch Ried Dürrau 2023 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Assertive spice-laden berry fruit nose. Lots of oak on the palate making it quite stern, but the fruit is good. 90/100

Weingut K+K Kirnbauer Blaufränkisch Mittelburgenland DAC Reserve Gold Reserve 2022 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Focused black cherry and blackberry fruit with some sour notes. Lively and intense with some lovely fruit, finishing sour. Lively and intense. 91/100

Weingut Silvia Heinrich Blaufränkisch Alte Reben 2019 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Juicy and vivid with some spicy oak. Firm and full with blackberry and black cherry fruit. Vivid and concentrated. 90/100

Weingut Kerschbaum Blaufränkisch Ried Dürrau 2019 Mittelburgenland, Austria
Supple and bright with nice, lively blackberry fruit. There’s some spicy character here, too. Nice weight. 91/100

Weingut Claus Preisinger Blaufränkisch ErDELuftGRAsundreBEN 2023 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Floral and inviting with elegant red cherry fruit on the nose. Super-fresh, pure palate is elegant, bright, expressive and red fruited. 95/100

Weingut Juris Tricata 2022 Neusiedlersee, Austria
A big, rich, ripe, oaky style with lots of slightly charred black cherry fruit. Lively. 90/100

Weingut Juris Blaufränkisch Ried Ungerberg 2021 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Fresh but still ripe, with red berries, spicy oak and nice intensity. It’s likeable in its style. 91/100

Weingut Paul Achs Blaufränkisch Ried Altenberg 2016 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Supple and ripe with nice detail and some structure under the fresh berry fruit. A little stern. 91/100

Weingut Umathum Blaufränkisch Ried Kirchberg 2015 Neusiedlersee, Austria
Supple and juicy with some spice and nice red fruits. Bright and pure with some nice structure. Finishes fresh with a sour cherry bite. 94/100

Weingut Esterházy Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Schildten 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Lovely floral nose with some brightness and purpose. Beautiful freshness and concentration here with red fruits to the fore. Nicely textured, and shows good acidity. 94/100

Weingut Nehrer Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Poschen 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Juicy and supple with nice acidity. Stylish red cherries and berries, finishing bright and fresh. Lovely stuff. 93/100

Weingut Remushof Jagschitz Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Ripe and rich, yet supple, with red berry and cherry fruit complemented by warm spiciness. Generous, ripe and sweetly fruited. 92/100

Weingut Ernst Triebaumer Blaufränkisch Ried Oberer Wald 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Floral aromatic nose with red cherries and berries. Nice weight. Juicy and grippy with lovely structure. 94/100

Weingut Ernst Triebaumer Blaufränkisch Ried Mariental 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
Fresh and juicy, and quite vivid, with nice grip. Lovely balance and focus here with some crunchy bite and lovely purity. 94/100

Weingut Feiler-Artinger Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Ruster Oberer Wald 2022 Leithaberg, Austria
Linear and structured with freshness. Substantial, classic style with some oak, and vivid, intense fruit. 93/100

Weingut Feiler-Artinger Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Ruster Ludmaisch 2022 Leithaberg, Austria
Spicy nose with some woody hints. The palate is vivid with nice sweet black fruits, some stern structure and a juicy, drying finish. 90/100

Weingut Prieler Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Pratschweingarten 2022 Leithaberg, Austria
Supple and fresh. Lively with some grip. Grainy and full with good structure, showing real intensity. Firm finish. 92/100

Weingut Gober & Freinbichler Blaufränkisch Ried Kirchholz 2021 Leithaberg, Austria
Floral and juicy, with brightness and freshness, as well as some ripeness to the raspberry and cherry fruit. Lighter in style but still has structure. 92/100

Weingut Stefan Zehetbauer Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Reckenschink 2021 Leithaberg, Austria
Aromatic, spicy black fruits nose. Sweet with some oak present. The palate is supple and juicy with nice graininess and lively balance. 92/100

Weingut Hans & Anita Nittnaus Blaufränkisch Ried Jungenberg 2021 Leithaberg, Austria
Juicy and lively with nice weight. This has real balance with lovely ripe berry fruit and then a tapering finish. A fresh, inviting style. 93/100

Weingut Hans & Anita Nittnaus Blaufränkisch Ried Altenberg 2016 Leithaberg, Austria
Warm spice-laden red berry fruits on the nose. The palate is supple with some wood spice and pepper framing the focused red fruits. 94/100

Weingut Gernot & Heike Heinrich Blaufränkisch Leithaberg DAC Ried Alter Berg 2019 Leithaberg, Austria
Fresh, vital and intense with lovely black cherry and blackcurrant fruit backed up by substantial tannin. Taut, reductive and intense, this is precise and mineralic. 95/100

Weingut Kollwentz Blaufränkisch Ried Point 2019 Leithaberg, Austria
Some spicy oak in the mix. A rich, ripe style but not jammy. Maybe the oak is a bit high in the mix here. 90/100

Weingut Erwin Tinhof Blaufränkisch Ried Kirchberg Gloriette 2015 Leithaberg, Austria
Supple and berryish with some evolution. Notes of herbs and earth. Mature and quite grainy. 88/100

Weingut Groszer Wein Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Szapary 2023 Eisenberg, Austria
Spicy, woody nose with sweet berry fruits. Earth and herbs here with some leafy overtones and a bit of wildness. 89/100

Weingut Thom Wachter Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Königsberg 2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Fresh and supple with nice acidity under the sweet red fruits. Berryish with some grip. 90/100

Weingut Thom Wachter Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Saybritz 2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Lively and balanced with sweet, focused red berry and cherry fruits showing nice grip. Has lovely sweet fruit but finishes fresh, with a slight saltiness on the finish. Stylish and vivid with a nice acid line. 94/00

Weingut Stubits Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Csaterberg 2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Supple and fine with nice brightness to the cherry and berry fruits. Has some grip. Lively stuff. 93/100

Weingut Krutzler Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Eisenberg  2022 Eisenberg, Austria
Nice freshness and intensity with a stern, spicy structural component. Juicy and lively with nice balance to the red berry fruits. 93/100

StephanO Das-Wein-Gut Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Saybritz 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Elegant and refined, but still showing good concentration to the black cherry and blackberry fruit. Finishes fresh with good structure and purity. 94/100

StephanO Das-Wein-Gut Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Ried Weinberg 2021 Eisenberg Austria
Aromatic nose with some woody hints to the fresh red cherry and black cherry fruit. The palate is lively and full, and fresh and intense, with good acidity. 92/100

Weingut Jalits Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Reihburg 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Lovely fresh red cherries and berries showing nice brightness, and lovely focused fruit, and good acidity. Concentrated and tannic, but refined, with a nice mineral twist. 94/100

Weingut Wachter-Wiesler Blaufränkisch Eisenberg DAC Reserve Ried Weinberg 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Lovely focus and freshness here with some reductive hints but also nice black cherry fruit with some sweetness and also good structural framing. 94/100

Weingut Uwe Schiefer Blaufränkisch Ried Szapary 2021 Eisenberg, Austria
Fresh and supple with a spicy overlay to the black fruits, and a grainy, slightly tarry edge and good acidity. 93/100

Weingut Uwe Schiefer, Blaufränkisch Ried Szapary 2014 Eisenberg , Austria
Supple and juicy with nice structure, showing some development with notes of herbs, earth and nice grip. This is really delicious. 93/100

title: Champagne Michel Gonet: one to watch, if they aren’t already on your radar
summary: Website: https://en.gonet.fr/champagne Michel Gonet have properties in Bordeaux, as well as their grower Champagne operation. They have a sizeable 38
content:
Website: https://en.gonet.fr/champagne

Michel Gonet have properties in Bordeaux, as well as their grower Champagne operation. They have a sizeable 38 hectares, spread out quite widely through the region, with vineyards in Côte des Blancs (these are Grand Cru), the Coteaux Sézannais, Montgueux, and Bar-sur-Aubois down in the south. Their winery and cellars are based in Avize.

Winemaking is in the hands of mother-and-daughter team Diane and Sophie Gonet, Chef de Cave Antoine Gomérieux. Marco Pelletier – owner of Vantre restaurant in Paris, who also makes wines at Bordeaux’s Domaine de Galouchey – is now a consultant here.

UK agent is Keeling Andrew

Champagne Michel Gonet 3 Terroirs Blanc de Blancs Extra Brut 2020 France
This was disgorged in 2025 after five years on lees with a dosage of 4 g/l, and shows a wide dynamic range, perhaps because it is from three very different terroirs (Le Mesnil sur Oger, Vindey, Montgueux). Great acid line under fine pear, apple and lemon fruit with some subtle toastiness. Really lively with a salty finish. 93/100

Champagne Michel Gonet Blanc de Blancs Grand Cru 2015 France
This is from Le Mesnil-sur-Oger, and the lieu-dit les Hautes Mottes. It’s zero dosage or up to 2 g/l depending on the vintage. Lively, fresh and linear with precise citrus fruit and good acidity. Lovely focus and amazing precision: this is really good. 95/100

Champagne Michel Gonet Blanc de Blancs Grand Cru 2016 France
Nice depth here, showing precision but also some weight. Lemon and pear fruit with some mineral detail. Lovely intensity and tension, with a long finish. 95/100

Champagne Michel Gonet Les Hautes Mottes Le Mesnil Sur Oger Grand Cru Brut Nature 2018 France
From the best part of Le Mesnil, this wine was initiated by Marco Pelletier and Raj Parr has also been involved in its creation. This site, next to Les Chétillons, is at the extreme end of the Côte des Blancs and has 30 cm topsoil and then mother rock. Base wine is fermented in oak barrels and then it spends five years on lees. Amazing depth with notes of toast, honey and spice alongside crystalline citrus fruit, with a slight saltiness. Real depth of citrus here with some white peach notes. Vinous. 96/100

title: Highlights: Casa Valduga Arte Tradicional Brut 2024 Brazil
summary: Website: https://loja.famigliavalduga.com.br/casa-valduga I’m in the lounge at Sao Paolo airport in Brazil, after an amazing few days doing a deep
content:
Website: https://loja.famigliavalduga.com.br/casa-valduga

I’m in the lounge at Sao Paolo airport in Brazil, after an amazing few days doing a deep dive into coffee at Fazenda Mio, which I’ll write up in full soon. I thought I’d try the only Brazilian wine on offer, and it’s an appealing and affordable sparkler. I’ve tasted quite a few of the higher-end Valduga wines before, but this was my first time with this less expensive one.

Casa Valduga Arte Tradicional Brut 2024 Brazil
This is a traditional method sparkler, 60% Chardonnay and 40% Pinot Noir, and it has spent a year on its lees. It’s fruity and bright and nicely aromatic, with sweet pear and cherry fruit with a touch of sweetness rounding things out nicely, and lovely vivid citrus notes. Juicy and quite tasty, with the focus firmly on the fruit. It’s not aiming at profundity, but pleasure, and it finishes with an appealing bitter hint that adds interest. 88/100 (in Brazil this retails for just over £10 equivalent)

title: United States of Blaufränkisch (2) Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Romania, Germany
summary: The second part of this epic tasting of Blaufränkisch takes me to Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Romania, Germany. Some
content:
The second part of this epic tasting of Blaufränkisch takes me to Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Romania, Germany. Some really nice surprises here. This leaves Austria for part 3, the largest contributor to this tasting.

  • Part 1, Hungary, is here
  • Part 2, Slovakia, Slovenia, Czechia, Italy, Poland, Germany, Romania, is here
  • Part 3, Austria, is here

SLOVAKIA

Slobodne Vinárstvo Alternativa 2017 Small Carpathians, Slovakia
Bright, open and expressive with lovely raspberry and cherry fruit, showing real elegance and drinkability. Distinctive and lovely. 93/100

Magula Rosenberg Frankovka 2021 Small Carpathians, Slovakia
Slightly wild spicy, herby nose with dusty notes. The palate is lively and juicy with a tart finish and spicy wildness again. 86/100

Sykora Fine Wines Frankovka Modrá black edition 2022 Small Carpathians, Slovakia
Warm and complex with textured strawberry and cherry fruit. Shows depth and some structure. Nicely expressive with lots of personality. 92/100

Bott Frigyes Frankovka Modrá 2023 Southern Slovakia, Slovakia
Classic in style with lovely vibrant cherry and berry fruits with nice acidity. Supple and lively with a good acid line. 93/100

Strekov 1075 Frankovka 2019 Southern Slovakia, Slovakia
Very spicy nose that’s quite vivid with some wildness. Tart raspberry and black cherry palate with some savoury phenolic notes. 87/100

Csernus Frankovka Modrá Selection 2016 Central Slovakia, Slovakia
Nicely aromatic with bright red fruits. Supple and nicely dense with fresh red cherry and berry fruits, finishing juicy. 92/100

SLOVENIA

Marof winery Modra Frankinja Mačkovci SVL Lega  2021 Podravje, Prekmurje, Slovenia
Wonderful aromas of sweet spice-laden black fruits. The palate has nice structure and weight with good balance. Real vitality and balance here. 94/100

Roka Winery Kog Blaufränkisch SVL Lega 2023 Podravje, Štajerska Slovenija, Slovenia
Good structure and weight with raspberry and cherry fruit. Juicy and grippy with a nice spicy overlay. Real substance. 94/100

Vino Greif Modra Frankinja Črešnjev Vrh 2023 Podravje, Štajerska Slovenija, Slovenia
Supple and elegant with nice vibrancy to the juicy raspberry and cherry fruit. Some real detail and elegance here. 94/100

Hiša vin Emino Modra Frankinja Eminent 2024 Podravje, Štajerska Slovenija, Slovenia
So attractive with sweet cherry and berry fruit, some ripe strawberry notes, and an openness. Quite lovely in a fruity style. 93/100

Vinska klet Šuklje Lodoma SVL Vas 2023 Posavje, Bela Krajina, Slovenia
Good acids and tannins under the sweet cherry and plum fruit. Pure and balanced. Nuanced with real quality. 94/100

Vinska klet Šuklje Vrbanjka SVL Lega 2021 Posavje, Bela Krajina, Slovenia
Supple cherries and raspberries with some grainy chalkiness, and slightly tarry tannins. Pure with real vitality. Elegant but substantial, ageing well. 95/100

Winery Klet Krško Modra Frankinja Turn Premium 2021Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
Good structure here. Vibrant cherry and raspberry fruit with good acidity. Fresh, spice-laden and tannic. Really vibrant. 93/100

Winery Klet Krško Modra Frankinja Turn Classic 2021 Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
Sweet floral aromatics on the nose. Juicy and bright and vivid on the palate with good acidity under the tart cherry and raspberry fruit, with lovely freshness. 93/100

Verstovšek Estate Blaufränkisch Classic 2021 Posavje, Bizeljsko-Sremič, Slovenia
